summary: Diagnostic trace log client segfaults (SIGSEGV) parsing malformed trace frames over the IVI log socket
description: On the IVI head unit, the diagnostic trace log consumer process (dlt-receive) crashes intermittently with SIGSEGV while draining the trace log socket. It happens when a peer streams a corrupt or truncated trace frame: the serial-header resync advances the receive buffer past the bytes that were actually received, so the message parser then reads the payload from an out-of-bounds offset. The native backtrace bottoms out in libdlt.so at dlt_message_read, called from dlt_client_main_loop, during the payload memcpy from the receive buffer. Reproducible under a noisy bus / lossy link when frame boundaries get shredded; the journal shows 'Complete message size too short' just before the fault. Expected: a malformed frame should be rejected (DLT_MESSAGE_ERROR_SIZE) and the connection resynced, not crash the client. Impact: loss of on-board diagnostics logging until the client is restarted.
